title: Job Specification for Communications and Engagement Intern  (paid internship)
description: **Overview** Codema, Dublin’s Energy Agency, is currently offering a very exciting six\-month paid internship across its Public and Stakeholder Engagement and Communications and Marketing teams. This internship provides a great opportunity for students or recent graduates in the area of Climate Change, Communications or a similar discipline to gain industry experience and apply what they have learnt in college. The intern will benefit from mentoring by Codema’s experienced staff while developing practical skills and gaining professional experience in one of Ireland’s most respected and leading not\-for\-profit organisations within the energy sector. The successful candidate will get the opportunity to work across several innovative projects and events, such as local community engagement on Decarbonising Zones, EU\-funded community\-focused projects as well as helping to support the organisation and roll\-out of public information events, demos, workshops and conferences. We are committed to leading Dublin's low\-carbon transition and we are looking for someone who is equally committed to these values and realises the key role that effective communication and local engagement plays in achieving this. Codema is a leading energy agency in Ireland, so this is a unique opportunity to gain experience and be at the forefront of driving awareness on the need to move Dublin away from fossil fuels through these various engagement activities and events. **What you will do** You will support the delivery of core communications and engagement outputs across both our Public and Stakeholder Engagement and Communications and Marketing teams. The role will involve tasks and opportunities to learn in areas such as community and stakeholder engagement, event planning, and communications and digital marketing. **Key tasks and responsibilities \- public and stakeholder engagement** ● On\-the\-ground/in\-person engagement with local communities and stakeholders. ○ Responsibilities include stakeholder mapping, preparing an engagement plan and tracking engagements, supporting the running of a local energy advice clinic. ● Support in the organisation of local engagement events and workshops. ○ Responsibilities include supporting Codema’s Public Engagement and Stakeholder Coordinator in designing workshop plans, organising venue and catering, supporting with facilitation, note\-taking and transcribing. ● Support in development and design of communications and engagement materials for local engagement projects ● Support in the integration of impact measurement tools to support engagement activities. ○ Responsibilities include research into suitable impact measurement tools and supporting the trial of relevant tools at engagement activities. ● Support in the creation of a detailed database of potential funding streams to support PASE. **Key tasks and responsibilities \- communications and marketing** ● Event coordination and delivery support ○ Support the Communications and Marketing team to plan, promote and deliver events, including conferences, workshops, EU project events and webinars. Responsibilities include managing registrations, liaising with speakers and sponsors, coordinating logistics and event materials, supporting promotion and stakeholder outreach, and helping ensure events run smoothly online and on\-site. ● Support Codema’s Home Energy Saving Kit and Think Energy campaign activities, including: ○ Providing event support to Codema’s Energy and Climate Awareness Officer, as needed, including at public information stands and Home Energy Saving Kit demonstrations ○ Providing administrative support for the Home Energy Saving Kit scheme, including responding to customer queries, fulfilling orders and managing stock ● Provide digital marketing support across Codema and Think Energy channels, including website, social media platforms and e\-newsletters. This will involve: ○ Creating engaging and creative content ○ Posting regularly across social media accounts ○ Posting news articles and updating website content ○ Assisting in the development of regular e\-newsletter ● Promotional material ○ Support the team with content creation and the design of promotional materials, including brochures, reports, information leaflets and infographics. **We are looking for** ● A team player who has excellent organisational and communication skills ● An eager learner who can hit the ground running ● Someone with a growth\-mindset who will grow and learn from working on real projects ● Someone who is not afraid to ask for help when they need it and will be always supported by the Codema team ● A people\-person with a real ‘can\-do’ attitude ● Outgoing and lots of positive energy **Minimum requirements** ● Currently studying or recently graduated with a Masters or Bachelor’s Degree in Climate Change/Marketing/Communications or similar discipline ● Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint ● Good communication skills with the ability to communicate effectively ● Excellent organisational and time management skills ● Fluency in English is essential ● Ability to work with a team and on own initiative ● Eligible to work in Ireland and available to start in June/July 2026 **Additional skills that would be a distinct advantage** ● Interest in the energy and climate landscape in Ireland ● Knowledge and experience of website Content Management Systems (e.g. Word Press, etc.) and social media platforms ● Experience with event organisation ● Knowledge and experience of design platforms such as Canva ● Experience working with a range of stakeholders **Location** The Codema office is located in Dublin city and is accessible by a range of sustainable transport modes. Secure bicycle parking is also available. The internship will be a mixture of remote and in\-office working, with in\-person work required for all events. **Duration** Six\-month full\-time paid internship, commencing June/July 2026\. **Salary** €2,469/month **Next steps** Please e\-mail your CV and a short cover letter to recruitment@codema.ie by 5pm, Friday, 15th May 2026\. As we may be advertising several positions, please insert the title of the position you are applying for in the subject line of your email. We aim to hold interviews the week of 25th May 2026\. **About Codema** We are Codema — Dublin’s Energy Agency. We are driven by our vision to positively impact people’s lives locally, while addressing the environmental damage caused by climate change at a global scale. We believe we can do this by improving the way we produce and use energy locally, while benefiting the people of Dublin. For more information, please visit our website at www.codema.ie. **Codema is an Equal Opportunities Employer**

title: Junior Accounts Payable Administrator
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC Utilities Group Ltd are seeking an experienced and detail\-oriented **Junior** **Accounts Payable Administrator** to join our growing Finance Team, based in Ballycoolin, Dublin 11\. This is a key role within the organisation, supporting the Finance Department in managing subcontractor accounts payable processes, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and compliance in all financial transactions. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Upload weekly subcontractor costs into Sage, creating purchase order cost accruals. Process large volumes of subcontractor invoices against accrued costs, deducting any necessary retentions. Maintain accurate subcontractor creditor balances at all times. Ensure all agreed credit terms are correctly recorded and utilised on the accounting system. Complete creditor statement reconciliations on a regular basis. Prepare weekly and monthly payment lists using aged creditor listings and notes on Sage 200 for review and approval. Notify payments through ROS and process any required RCT deductions within Sage. Review and adjust accrued costs to ensure accuracy. Handle supplier and subcontractor queries promptly and professionally. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Proven experience in an Accounts Payable or Finance Administration role. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across departments. Flexible approach and ability to manage variable workloads. Strong IT skills, particularly Microsoft Excel (advanced level) and Sage 200 experience (desirable). Fluent English, both written and verbal. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"*. Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior Ecologist
description: The Junior Ecologist supports the delivery of ecological surveys, assessments, and reporting across a wide range of infrastructure, energy, renewables, and development projects. The role provides an opportunity to develop core consultancy skills while working as part of multidisciplinary project teams under the guidance of senior and principal ecologists. **Key Responsibilities** **Field Survey Support** * Assist with ecological field surveys including habitat surveys, protected species surveys, and monitoring work (e.g. GCN, bats, birds, otter, water vole – depending on experience and season). * Support data collection, recording, and interpretation in line with recognised survey guidance. * Follow health, safety, and biosecurity procedures during fieldwork. **Assessment \& Reporting** * Support the preparation of technical reports, including: + Ecological Impact Assessments (EcIA) + EIA ecology chapters + Baseline reports and survey summaries * Assist with data analysis, mapping, and presentation of findings. * Contribute to mitigation measures and recommendations under supervision. **Project Support** * Work within project teams to deliver ecology inputs to agreed scope, programme, and budget. * Assist with project administration, scheduling surveys, and coordination with colleagues. * Support senior staff in responding to client queries and regulator requests. **Learning \& Development** * Develop technical knowledge of UK wildlife legislation, planning policy, and best‑practice guidance. * Participate in training, mentoring, and technical development activities. * Build competency towards independent survey and reporting capability. **Collaboration \& Culture** * Work collaboratively with ecologists, EIA practitioners, planners, and other specialists. * Contribute positively to team culture and knowledge sharing. * Uphold ERM values, quality standards, and commitment to sustainability. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Degree in Ecology, Environmental Science, or a related discipline. * Basic understanding of UK ecology, habitats, and wildlife legislation. * Willingness to undertake fieldwork, including early starts and occasional travel. * Strong attention to detail and good written communication skills. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team. * Full UK driving licence (or working towards). **Desirable** * Some experience of ecological field surveys (e.g. through placements, volunteering, or previous roles). * Familiarity with ecological survey methods and reporting. * Interest in developing specialist skills (e.g. ornithology, freshwater ecology, protected species). * Membership (or working towards membership) of a relevant professional body (e.g. CIEEM). * Basic GIS or data handling skills. **Behaviours \& Attributes** * Curious and keen to learn. * Reliable, organised, and proactive. * Positive attitude to feedback and development. * Commitment to high standards of safety and quality. * Passion for ecology and environmental protection. **Reporting \& Support** * Reports to: Senior Ecologist / Principal Ecologist / Ecology Team Lead * Works closely with: CPD project teams, EIA practitioners, and other technical disciplines **Why Join ERM as a Junior Ecologist** This role offers structured development within a leading global environmental consultancy, exposure to nationally significant projects, and a clear pathway for progression to Consultant and Senior Ecologist roles. Candidates may be invited to complete an online assessment as part of our recruitment process. All personal information will be handled confidentially and in compliance with data protection laws. ⠀ ERM is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive, it’s an essential part of what makes ERM a great place to build a career and helps us create better solutions for our clients. We welcome talent from all backgrounds and provide equal opportunities for every candidate. If you have a disability, are neurodivergent, or need accommodations during the selection process, we’re here to support you. Our commitment doesn’t stop at hiring. Once you join us, we’ll ensure you have the tools, support, and adjustments needed to succeed and feel a true sense of belonging. Learn more about our Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging (DIB) efforts by visiting our website or exploring our 2025 Sustainability Report..

title: Junior Field IT Support
description: **Job Summary** **JOB DESCRIPTION – Junior Field IT Support** Location:Dublin (Onsite) Division: Ticketmaster UK/IRE – Enterprise Support Contract Terms: Permanent, 40 hours per week **THE TEAM** The Technology Support team which is part of the Global Support \& Operations organisation, is a customer focused service delivery team providing technical support and installations services across the various business units and client base. We are a key department responsible for the successful delivery of major events and festivals as well as ensuring the ongoing support and maintenance of our fixed client base and owned and operated venues. Focused on providing high levels of customer and client satisfaction we are driven to provide safe, secure and reliable solutions and services that enables fans to enjoy the experience of live events. **THE JOB** The Technology Support team will focus their day to day by providing support to our clients and the wider Ticketmaster and Livenation business either remotely or in person. Supporting a wide range of products, peripherals and Ticketmaster proprietary software you will need to be technically focused and customer centric. The Technical Support team will often work in the pressurised but enjoyable environment of festivals and outdoor events installing a wide range of hardware and software solutions that provides fans with a seamless and enjoyable experience attending Live events. **What You Will Be Trained To Support** * Installation and support of Ticketmaster proprietary software and hardware e.g. Ticket printers, Handheld Devices and Point of Sale * Installation of software, firmware to ensure devices are up to date * Installing and supporting network devices including Wireless Access Points, Switches, Bridges etc * Upgrading and replacing hardware and software as and when required * Configuring and testing of the above said hardware and software * Diagnosing and remediation of issues and problems both remotely and on\-site * Raising incidents and updating incident management software * Tracking Assets and updating asset management software * Minimise incidents and service distribution by providing proactive support and maintenance * Coordinating and liaising with clients prior to attendance and installations * Working with third party providers and contractors often in a lead capacity * Provide on\-site end user training and demonstration of Ticketmaster products * Updating and maintaining technical and operational documentation * Communicating and collaborating with multiple teams and clients * Following best practices and procedures ensuring standards are maintained * Maintain an understanding of all TM and LN products usage and functionality **What You Need To Know** * A willingness or keen interest or knowledge of Microsoft, and mobile device applications. * An interest in learning wired and wireless networking technologies and products * Eager to expand knowledge within the entertainment business and its requirements to meet our client’s needs, be committed to your development within the Junior support role. **YOU** * An aptitude for understanding concepts and methodologies, both technical and non\-technical * Good interpersonal and communication skills – Fluent English essential * Candidate must be detail oriented and organised * Ability to travel required * May be required to work nights, weekends, or holidays * Must have valid clean driving licence * Candidate must be dynamic to meet the needs of an ever\-changing live entertainment environment **TICKETMASTER VALUES** * Reliability – We understand that fans and clients rely on us to power their live event experiences, and we rely on each other to make it happen. * Teamwork \- – We believe individual achievement pales in comparison to the level of success that can be achieved by a team * Integrity – We are committed to the highest moral and ethical standards on behalf of the countless partners and stakeholders we represent * Belonging \- We are committed to building a culture in which all people can be their authentic selves, have an equal voice and opportunities to thrive **EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES** We are passionate and committed to our people and go beyond the rhetoric of diversity and inclusion. You will be working in an inclusive environment and be encouraged to bring your whole self to work. We will do all that we can to help you successfully balance your work and home life. As a growing business we will encourage you to develop your professional and personal aspirations, enjoy new experiences, and learn from the talented people you will be working with. It’s talent that matters to us and we encourage applications from people irrespective of their gender, race, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability status or caring responsibilities. **CULTURE** We’re fans who help fans everywhere get in to the live events they love. A proud division of Live Nation Entertainment, Ticketmaster has changed the way the world connects with their favourite artists, teams, and shows, and we continue to shape innovation every day. We’re not just selling tickets (though we do that better than anyone else), we’re enriching lives one amazing experience at a time. And we think that’s pretty amazing. If you’re passionate about the magic of live entertainment like we are, and you want to work at a company dedicated to helping millions of fans experience it, we want to hear from you. We have a ‘Fan First’ mindset, placing customers at the heart of everything we do. Whether working behind the scenes or engaging directly with the ‘Fan’ you will be part of a culture that supports and enriches the fan experience. Your work, our passion… delivering world class experiences – Fan First. Live Nation Entertainment will never request payment or equipment purchases as part of the hiring process. Recruiters will only contact candidates from official Live Nation or affiliated brand email domains.

title: Junior Pensions Consultant
description: **Junior Pensions Consultant** **About Your New Employer** Join a leading provider of pensions solutions and be part of an innovative and dynamic organisation focused on employee engagement and investment offerings. As Junior Pensions Consultant you will have the opportunity to work within a team dedicated to supporting thousands across the construction industry with pension, life assurance, and illness benefits. **About Your New Job** * As Junior Pensions Consultant you will deliver engaging presentations (in\-person and virtual) to pension scheme members across multiple schemes. * You will explain pension benefits, options, and retirement processes in a clear, accessible way and help increase member understanding. * You will support member engagement campaigns, respond to follow\-up queries, and promote Additional Voluntary Contributions (AVCs). * As Junior Pensions Consultant you will tailor your communications for different audiences (e.g., site staff, pre\-retirees), maintaining high levels of accuracy and clarity, and collaborate with internal teams to ensure consistency and support the development of communication materials. **What Skills You Need** * QFA qualified * Strong presentation and public speaking skills, with the ability to explain complex topics simply. * Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities, confident with both large and small groups. * Organised and goal\-oriented, able to manage your own diary and work to deadlines. * Relevant experience in pensions, financial services, or employee benefits (preferred). **What's on Offer** The chance to make a real impact by enhancing financial literacy and future readiness for members in a critical sector. Excellent professional development opportunities in a supportive and collaborative environment. Activity target driven role with a focus on engagement and education, not sales or regulated advice. **What's Next** Apply now by submitting your application or contact Aoife Lowe, 01 474 4643, alowe@sigmar.ie. to take your next career step. We welcome candidates seeking a rewarding and meaningful role in a unique, supportive environment.

title: Junior Sales Representative - Trade Counter Retail
description: **About Us:** Established in 2002, Concrete Care has become a leading specialist in the sale and hire of a diverse range of surface preparation and dust extraction equipment. Our extensive product lineup includes concrete floor grinders and polishers, industrial buffers and burnishers, scarifiers and shavers, floor scrapers, shot blasters, industrial vacuums, forced action mixers, power floats, handheld power tools, electric mini dumpers, and more. We pride ourselves on delivering top\-notch customer service and maintaining a great team environment. **Job Description:** We are seeking a dynamic and customer\-focused Sales Advisor to join our showroom team in Ballyfermot, Dublin. The ideal candidate will play a crucial role in driving sales, providing exceptional customer service, and ensuring smooth operations within the showroom. **Please note** **that this position cannot consider overseas applications.** **Responsibilities:** * Assist customers in the showroom with product inquiries and sales, offering knowledgeable and professional advice. * Make outbound calls to potential and existing customers to drive sales and provide product information. * Handle customer orders over the phone and via email efficiently and accurately. * Place orders with suppliers and manage incoming stock deliveries. * Maintain stock levels in the showroom, ensuring products are displayed attractively and restocked as needed. * Support the showroom team with inventory management and stock\-taking activities. * Provide excellent customer service, addressing customer queries and resolving issues promptly. * Utilise computer systems for order processing, inventory management, customer communications, and CRM software. * Collaborate with the team to achieve sales targets and contribute to a positive work environment. **Requirements:** * Proficiency in computer skills, including experience with inventory management, sales, and CRM software. * Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal. * Strong customer service orientation with a friendly and professional demeanour. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Previous experience in a tool hire shop or counter sales is preferred. * Good organisational skills and attention to detail. * Reliable and punctual with a strong work ethic. **Benefits:** * Competitive salary and potential for commission. * On\-site parking available. * Supportive and friendly team environment. * Opportunity to work with a leading company in the concrete preparation and flooring industry. If you are passionate about sales and customer service and have an interest in the construction and flooring industry, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your CV and cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are a perfect fit for this role. Concrete Care is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €30,000\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: Junior Sous Chef
description: **The Mespil Hotel has an exciting opportunity for a** **Junior Sous Chef** **to join our Kitchen** team. The main responsibilities of this role is to support the Head Chef and Sous Chef in the day\-to\-day operation of the kitchen, ensuring high standards of food quality, hygiene, and presentation are consistently delivered. The Junior Sous Chef plays a key role in supervising kitchen staff and maintaining a smooth and efficient service. This is a challenging position for a highly motivated, hardworking individual interested in developing themselves in the hospitality industry. Areas of responsibility will focus on the following areas: **Kitchen Operations:** * Assist in the preparation, cooking, and presentation of dishes to the highest standard * Support the Sous Chef and Head Chef in managing kitchen operations during service * Supervise and guide Commis Chefs and Chef de Parties * Ensure all food is prepared in line with recipes, portion control, and presentation standards * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe kitchen environment in line with HACCP standards * Monitor stock levels and assist with ordering and inventory control * Minimise food waste and support cost control initiatives * Ensure compliance with all food safety, health \& safety, and hygiene regulations * Assist in training and development of junior kitchen team members * Step in to lead a section or service in the absence of senior chefs * Assist with stock control, ordering, and inventory management * Minimise waste and ensure efficient use of ingredients * Work efficiently in a fast\-paced environment * Maintain a professional appearance and attitude * Adhere to all company policies and procedures ### **Candidate Requirements** ### **Key Requirements:** * Previous experience as a Chef de Partie or similar role in a busy kitchen * Strong knowledge of food preparation, cooking techniques, and kitchen operations * HACCP and food safety certification * Ability to work under pressure in a fast\-paced environment and meet deadlines * Strong organisational and time management skills * Team player with good communication and leadership ability * Passion for food, quality, and continuous improvement ### **Desirable:** * Formal culinary qualification * Experience in hotel or similar kitchen environments * Knowledge of menu development and cost control ### **Key Competencies:** * Attention to detail * Leadership and teamwork * Flexibility and adaptability * Commitment to quality and standards * Strong work ethic and reliability * Good communication and teamwork skills ### **Personal Attributes** * Passion for food and hospitality * Positive, can\-do attitude * Reliable, punctual, and professional * Willingness to learn and develop ### **Working Conditions:** * Shift work including evenings, weekends, and public holidays * Physically demanding role requiring long periods of standing **The benefits of working at the Mespil Hotel:** * Competitive Salary * Continuous training and professional development programs. * Complimentary stays at our sister property. * Additional days off based on your length of service. * Access to our Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for you and your family. * Option to join our Pension Scheme after 1 year of service. * Option to avail of Health Insurance at reduced rates. * Complimentary buffet\-style meals in the staff canteen for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. * A referral program\-refer a friend to join our team and receive a reward. * Participation in the Bike 2 Work Scheme and the Tax Saver Commuter Ticket Scheme. * Employee recognition initiatives to celebrate your achievements. * An exciting calendar of social events. * Being part of a supportive and dynamic team in a hotel recognized as an "Outstanding Employer" for 2023, 2024, 2025 and 2026 through the Failte Ireland Employer Excellence Program

title: Junior Structural Engineer
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Our Building Engineering team sits at the heart of Arup's commitment to sustainable development. We engineer innovative solutions that minimize environmental impact and create resilient, sustainable structures. **The Opportunity** Join our Building Engineering team in Cork and contribute to impactful projects across Ireland and Europe. This role provides an excellent opportunity for career development and the chance to work on cutting\-edge structural engineering projects. **As a Structural Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Undertake structural analysis using GSA, Tekla Structural Designer, or other structural software. * Complete the design of structural elements in reinforced concrete, steel, timber, or masonry. * Prepare mark\-ups and work with structural technicians to produce structural drawings using Revit. * Represent Arup at meetings with the client, other consultants and/or contractors. * Conduct site visits and structural investigations. * Prepare reports, presentations, and site reports. * Collaborate with the Design Team on Common Data Environments such as ACC. **Does this role inspire you?** We are seeking a highly motivated and technically proficient individual with a passion for structural engineering and a commitment to sustainable design. **Candidates Must Have** * 1 \- 3 years of post \- graduate experience in structural design. * Degree qualified in Civil/Structural Engineering or similar. MEng or equivalent preferred . **We Also Value** * Excellent communication and report writing skills, including the ability to communicate technical concepts clearly and effectively. * Proficiency in relevant design software packages . * Interest in using and developing digital design tools to deliver smart solutions. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Competitive benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing program. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. \#IREGrad **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Lab Validation Analyst
description: Lab System Validation Analyst (Contract) **Location:** On\-site, Dublin, Ireland **Contract rate:** €525 per day **Contract length:** Start ASAP through 30/06/2026, with extensions subject to project SOW Role overview This contract role supports the validation and ongoing compliance of computerised laboratory systems within a regulated GxP environment. The focus is on ensuring laboratory systems are fit for purpose, compliant with regulatory expectations, and maintained in a validated state throughout their lifecycle. You’ll work closely with Quality, IT, and Laboratory Operations teams, taking ownership of validation activities across a range of lab systems and instruments. This is a delivery\-focused role suited to someone comfortable operating on\-site in a regulated laboratory setting, with clear accountability for validation outcomes. Key responsibilities * Lead and execute computer system validation (CSV) activities for laboratory instruments, software, and analytical platforms, including CDS, LIMS, ELN, and SDMS. * Author, review, and maintain validation documentation such as URS, FRS, risk assessments, traceability matrices, IQ/OQ/PQ protocols, validation plans, and final reports. * Ensure compliance with GAMP 5, EU Annex 11, 21 CFR Part 11, and ALCOA\+ data integrity principles. * Support system changes through impact assessments, change control, and re\-validation activities. * Work collaboratively with QA, IT, and laboratory stakeholders to agree validation strategies, priorities, and timelines. * Maintain the validated state through periodic reviews, audit preparation, and inspection support. * Participate in vendor qualification and audits where required. * Provide practical guidance to system owners and users on validation processes and regulatory expectations. What we’re looking for * Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline. * 3\+ years’ experience performing CSV or lab system validation within a GxP\-regulated laboratory environment. * Hands\-on experience with lab informatics systems such as Waters Empower, Agilent OpenLab, LabVantage, MasterControl, or similar. * Strong working knowledge of GAMP 5, EU Annex 11, 21 CFR Part 11, and data integrity requirements. * Experience using validation documentation and testing tools such as HP ALM, ValGenesis, or equivalent. * Solid understanding of laboratory workflows and instrument qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ). Who the role is (and isn’t) for This role suits an experienced validation professional who is comfortable working on\-site, taking ownership of their own deliverables, and engaging directly with Quality and laboratory teams. The work is hands\-on and documentation\-heavy, with a strong emphasis on compliance, traceability, and audit readiness. It is unlikely to suit candidates seeking a fully remote role, a purely strategic or advisory position, or those without direct experience validating laboratory systems in regulated environments.

title: Legal Counsel Europe IB
description: **Overview** PepsiCo's Law and Compliance \& Ethics function helps drive business objectives and sustainable results while protecting PepsiCo—legally, ethically, and reputationally. We align with the business, with dedicated lawyers and legal professionals supporting each Sector and BU/market, alongside Global Group/Corporate specialists covering practice areas and governance needs. We work Faster, Stronger, Better by leveraging technology, simplifying processes, and expanding capabilities—while advancing diversity and inclusion within our department and the broader legal profession. By joining the award winning Legal team supporting International Beverages Europe (IB Europe) as Legal Counsel – Europe IB (Franchise), you will be an integral member of the Europe Legal team, providing legal support for our Franchise (FOBO) beverage markets in Europe (excluding Russia), and assisting the General Counsel across all FOBO territories. **Role Purpose** Provide proactive, business centric legal counsel across Europe FOBO markets—acting as a strategic partner to franchise leadership, safeguarding PepsiCo through robust governance, practical risk management, and high quality contracting—while contributing to Europe wide initiatives and standards. **Reporting line:** Reports to the General Counsel, Europe IB **Responsibilities** * Strategic business partnering: Drive timely, high quality legal support for franchise operations; proactively analyze and mitigate risk while enabling growth. * Primary legal point of contact: Serve franchise leaders (Franchise Directors/Managers) and Finance/Control teams as first line independent counsel on day to day matters. * Litigation strategy \& management: Under the General Counsel's supervision, oversee external counsel, set litigation strategy, and implement mitigation plans. * Franchise governance \& transactions: Draft, negotiate, and manage core franchise agreements and amendments, including Bottling Appointments, Concentrate \& Marketing Agreements, and Business Development Agreements. * Commercial contracting: Issue accurate, reliable contracts across franchise, control, supply chain, sales, and finance—aligned to PepsiCo policies, precedents, and controls. * Risk \& corporate governance: Identify and manage legal risk/exposure with third parties; ensure robust corporate governance and complete corporate records. * Policy implementation \& enablement: Implement company policies and provide cross functional support on the documentation needed for effective execution. * Digital \& process excellence: Simplify/automate legal processes; drive the continued roll out of digital tools across Europe IB. * Training \& capability building: Design and deliver targeted training (e.g., anti bribery/anti corruption, antitrust/competition, key contract provisions); uplift legal acumen across client groups. * Collaboration across IB system: Partner with Europe OU Functions, IB Legal, Global Law Department, PPGA, and Trademark teams to apply global precedents and best practices. **Qualifications** **Minimum Qualifications:** * LL.B. (Bachelor of Laws) required; LL.M. is an advantage. * Qualified lawyer, admitted in country of qualification, with minimum 8 years' experience (law firm and/or in house). * FCPA and EU Competition Law: required. * GDPR/data privacy: knowledge is a plus (and familiarity with evolving European privacy frameworks). * Demonstrated experience in corporate compliance, drafting/reviewing/negotiating a broad range of commercial contracts, and stakeholder management across complex, multi market environments. * Fluency in English (spoken \& written); fluency in other EU languages is an advantage. * Ability to prioritize multiple matters to meet business needs; strong computer literacy, negotiation, and communication skills. * Professional, mature, and effective under pressure in fast paced environments; comfortable partnering with senior and junior colleagues. * Team player with a collaborative mindset. **Preferred Skills:** * Strong attention to detail, analytical rigor, and resourcefulness; significant autonomy in decision making with limited supervision. * Self starter who leads projects and delivers on time with clear stakeholder engagement. * Demonstrated leadership—able to work independently and within a collaborative team. * High learning agility—quickly understands local law/regulatory developments and PepsiCo's business; learns from external best practices and cascades knowledge across the team. * Excellent interpersonal, project management, time management, and organizational skills. * Excellent command of computer skills, negotiation and communication skills. * Professional, mature, able to work under pressure in a fast\-paced environment. * Team player who is flexible and confident working with both senior and junior colleagues.

title: Maintenance Contract Sales/Internal Sales
description: **Job Title** **Service \& Maintenance Contracts Sales Executive** (Construction / Automation \& Access Systems) **Department** Sales working with Service / Maintenance Team **Reports To** Sales Director *with strong day\-to\-day liaison with* Service \& Maintenance Manager **Role Summary** This role is responsible for growing and managing revenue from service and maintenance contracts across our installed base of automated gates/doors, barriers, blockers/bollards, folding gates, car parking systems, CCTV and access control. proposals follow internal approval processes (pricing, margin, terms, credit checks where required). * Work closely with service scheduling, engineers, parts/admin and finance to ensure smooth onboarding and billing. **Products / Systems Covered** Automated gates and door automation, folding gates, barriers, blockers/bollards, car parking systems, CCTV, access control and associated safety devices and controls. **Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Proven experience in B2B sales / account management (construction, service/maintenance, technical products or similar). * Confident in outbound calling and structured follow\-up of warm leads (recent installs). * Strong written communication (clear quotes/proposals) and numeracy (pricing, margin). * Highly organised: able to manage multiple customer follow\-ups, quotes and stakeholders. * Comfortable visiting sites and engaging customers in construction/FM environments. * CRM competence and good Microsoft Office skills. * Determination to succeed and strong interest in closing sales * Full driving licence. **Desirable** * Experience with service agreements/PPM/SLA sales. * Knowledge of automation/security systems (gates/doors/barriers/access control/CCTV) or similar electro\-mechanical systems. * Familiarity with construction/FM processes (inductions, permits, RAMS interfaces, contractor requirements). **Behaviours / competencies** * Proactive and persistent; strong follow\-through. * Customer\-focused, professional, and solution oriented. * Collaborative: works well with sales and service operations. * Detail\-oriented: scopes and exclusions are clear; minimal contract disputes later. **Working Hours \& Package** · Hours: 9am – 5pm Monday to Friday * Salary: Depending on Experience. * Travel: mileage reimbursement or pool car use per policy if available * Laptop * EAP \& Pension (after probation) * 21 days holidays We are looking to grow our Sales Team. As travel would be required as part of this role a drivers license and access to a car would be preferred. At Spectrum we want to offer you a career, not just a job. title: Power BI Business Analyst - Dunboyne, Co. Meath (ON-SITE)
description: **IT \- Head Office, Dunboyne, Co. Meath** We are seeking a highly capable Power BI Business Analyst who can take reporting requirements from initial concept through to fully polished, accurate, and insight driven Power BI reports. This role requires a strong blend of business analysis, data literacy, stakeholder engagement, UX awareness, and quality assurance. **This role is primarily on\-site. Our head office is not serviced reliably by public transport so possessing your own transport is a requirement.** **The successful candidate will work in close partnership with a dedicated Power BI Developer/Report Writer, ensuring solutions are robust, scalable, and aligned to business needs.** **Responsibilities:** * Requirements Gathering \& Stakeholder Engagement Engage with business users to understand reporting needs, KPIs, and decision\-making goals. Facilitate workshops, interviews, and discovery sessions to translate highlevel requirements into clear reporting specifications. Challenge assumptions and clarify ambiguous requirements to ensure reports add genuine value. Act as the bridge between the business and the technical Power BI development function. Lead the creation of a clear, structured BRD (business requirements document) that captures business objectives, KPIs, user needs, and acceptance criteria in a way that fully reflects the reporting needs of the business. * Report Design \& Conceptualisation Create conceptual wireframes, mock\-ups, and storyboards outlining report layout, data flow, and user experience. Define business logic, metric definitions, and DAX calculations required to support the report. Ensure reports follow best practices in visual design, usability, accessibility, and data storytelling. * Collaboration with Power BI Developers Work with the Power BI Developer to translate business requirements into a comprehensive Functional Requirements Document (FRD) that clearly defines the required functional logic, data structures, and Power BI behaviours. This includes producing a detailed functional specification covering report layouts and wireframes; data fields, definitions and transformation rules; required DAX measures; user interactions, filters and UX flows; acceptance criteria; and all relevant data sources and validation rules. Work collaboratively throughout development sprints to validate interim builds. Work with the BI developer to confirm feasibility and ensure all functional requirements are aligned with Power BI's capabilities, datamodel designprinciples, and performance considerations. * Data Validation \& Quality Assurance Validate report outputs against source systems to ensure complete accuracy. Perform data sanity checks, anomaly detection, and metric verification. Conduct end\-to\-end UAT with stakeholders, ensuring reports meet functional and business expectations before release. * Documentation \& Governance Produce high\-quality documentation including requirements, definitions, data dictionaries, and user guides. Implement a governance framework for Power BI reporting, including a reporting catalogue, classification standards, and controlled processes for report creation, approval, and lifecycle management * User Training \& Adoption Support rollout of new dashboards through demonstrations, training sessions, and Q\&A. Gather feedback post deployment to identify enhancements and ensure continued value. **Required Knowledge and Skills:** Proven experience as a **Business Analyst** within data analytics or BI environments. Strong understanding of Power BI concepts including visual design, data modelling basics, and DAX fundamentals. Ability to create clear wireframes, prototypes, and conceptual dashboard designs. Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and workshop facilitation skills. Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and identify meaningful insights. Experience working closely with technical Power BI developers or data engineers. **Experience Required:** 5 years experience in a similar position Knowledge of SQL and data querying for validation/troubleshooting. Experience with broader Microsoft BI stack (Azure, Synapse, Analysis Services). Understanding of BI governance frameworks and KPI standardisation. Background in process improvement, data literacy initiatives, or change management. Experience in engaging with senior leadership and providing strategic insights, translating business requirements into solution design **What You Get:** Kilsaran is an organization that believes that our people are our greatest strength,and our success is down to the dedication and enthusiasm of our team. As such, we have dedicated benefits structures to reward our team. The benefits on offer for this position are as follows: Competitive Annual Salary Annual Bonus Defined Contribution Pension Scheme Healthcare Subsidy Twenty\-one Days Annual Leave **Additional Benefits:** Employee Assistance Programme Cycle to Work Scheme Sick Pay **Hiring Process Upon Application:** Telephone Screening 2 round interview process (1st Round can be completed via MS Teams where necessary) Offer Letter to Successful candidate

title: Process Technician Apprentice
description: **Process Technician Apprenticeship** ===================================== ***Are you looking to kick start a career as a Process Technician and get an education while getting paid?!*** -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- If so, Pfizer Grange Castle has an apprenticeship opportunity within a state\-of\-the\-art Biopharmaceutical manufacturing plant for you! *In partnership with the Irish MedTech Association \& IBEC our Engineering network within Pfizer Grange Castle we are seeking candidates for a Process* *Technician Apprenticeship.* *This is a 2\-year program combining part time learning and work placement, leading to attainment of Higher Cert (Level 6\) in Bio Process and Chemical Process Technician.* This programme includes both technical and academic content with a key focus on manufacturing. This is an opportunity to join a world class facility who is dedicated to bringing therapies that will significantly improve patients’ lives. We are looking for an **Apprentice Process Technician** to join our team, this role will support manufacturing operations by assisting with routine production activities, including equipment preparation, material handling, in‑process checks, basic equipment verification. The role includes supporting sampling activities, preparation of materials or solutions for manufacturing use, stock control, and maintenance of production equipment and tools. This role is an on the job, off the job learning. **You will be required to attend college for 15 weeks block release at TU Dublin** **You will return to Pfizer for 9 months. When onsite in Pfizer Grange Castle, you will work on a Shift Pattern which may include Days \& Nights. This will be discussed in advance!** **About the Role and** **Responsibilities:** You will be directly involved in the manufacturing of our products, ensuring that they meet all specifications for safety, potency, and efficacy. You will work within the guidelines of current Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) to ensure conformance with all regulatory requirements and site Standard Operation Procedures (SOP’s), while assisting in the improvement of all current processes through our Continuous Improvement Program. * Assist in maintaining a safety orientated culture, cGMP compliant work environment always. * Perform initial troubleshooting of issues identified during routing operations. * Assist with the investigation of operational deviations through Quality System, engaging with relevant functions as appropriate. * Provide input into the creation and maintenance of area SOPs and batch records. * Preparation of materials for batches and batch manufacture of bio pharmaceutical products. * Use Lean tools as part of daily operations (e.g., Method 1, 5S) to optimise efficiency and drive culture of Continuous Improvement and Zero Defects. * Assist in facility start up, installation and validation activities to support the introduction of new products * **This is a shift\-based role (For 24/7 Operations), Day and Night shifts.** **Candidate requirements:** * Minimum Leaving Certificate. * Have a passion for Science and/or Technology, Engineering. * Demonstrate a strong work ethic. * Work well as part of a team. * Self\-motivated / self\-starter * Excellence in systems and practical applications. * Have good time management \& prioritisation skills * Be committed to excellence in operational and compliance requirements * Focused on process improvements within a structured environment. * Willingness to learn and grow. * **Willing to do Shift Work.** * Willing to start **September 2026** **Additional Information** * In order to be considered for this position in Ireland you need to be legally eligible to work in Ireland. * Please note there is no relocation support available for this position **How to apply** * Make a difference today, all suitable candidates should apply with CV below. We are looking forward to hearing from you! Purpose **Breakthroughs that change patients' lives**... At Pfizer we are a patient centric company, guided by our four values: courage, joy, equity and excellence. Our breakthrough culture lends itself to our dedication to transforming millions of lives. Digital Transformation Strategy One bold way we are achieving our purpose is through our company wide digital transformation strategy. We are leading the way in adopting new data, modelling and automated solutions to further digitize and accelerate drug discovery and development with the aim of enhancing health outcomes and the patient experience. Equal Employment Opportunity We believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial to building a successful business. As an employer, Pfizer is committed to celebrating this, in all its forms – allowing for us to be as diverse as the patients and communities we serve. Together, we continue to build a culture that encourages, supports and empowers our employees. Disability Inclusion Our mission is unleashing the power of all our people and we are proud to be a disability inclusive employer, ensuring equal employment opportunities for all candidates. We encourage you to put your best self forward with the knowledge and trust that we will make any reasonable adjustments to support your application and future career. Your journey with Pfizer starts here! Support Services

title: Procurement & EPOS Assistant
description: The Wright Group is looking for a Procurement \& EPOS Assistant to support our purchasing and EPOS systems across multiple hospitality venues. This is an entry\-level / early career role suited to someone with some hospitality experience who is looking to move into a head office support function. You will work as part of a team, assisting with day\-to\-day tasks across systems, purchasing, and supplier administration. Full training will be provided. Key Responsibilities **EPOS Systems Management** * Update and maintain EPOS systems across all hospitality venues. * Manage product file changes including PLUs, menus, categories, pricing, and promotions. * Ensure accuracy between EPOS, back\-office, and stock systems. * Coordinate EPOS rollouts across multiple venues. * Troubleshoot issues and liaise with IT or external providers. * Support new system features and integrations. **Procurement \& Purchasing Support** * Assist with supplier ordering, sourcing, and stock processes. * Maintain accurate purchasing records. * Ensure correct pricing and supplier data in all systems. * Monitor stock levels and support decisions. **Supplier Contract Management** * Maintain and oversee supplier contracts. * Verify compliance with pricing, service levels, deliveries, and obligations. * Escalate discrepancies. * Support contract reviews and renewals. **Internal Controls \& Reporting** * Maintain strong control procedures. * Produce purchasing, stock, and EPOS reports. * Assist audits and compliance checks. * Support continuous improvement initiatives. **Skills, Knowledge \& Experience** **Essential:** * Previous hospitality industry experience (any role). * Strong analytical and numerical skills. * Excellent attention to detail. * Strong organisational and communication abilities. * Willingness to travel across multiple venues. **Desirable:** * Full driving licence. **Why work for the group:** * Employee Assistance Program * Fun staff days out * % off our sister sites * Superb training * Competitive salary * Employee Recognition Award * Employee of the month * Bike to work * Strong company culture * Staff referral program * Great opportunities for career progression

title: Product Analyst
description: **Base Location:** You'll be expected to spend 50% of your working week in one of the following locations : Dublin, Reading, Havant, Inverness, Aberdeen, Glasgow and Perth **Salary:** €49,400 \- €74,200 performance\-related bonus and a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Salary:** £49,004 \- £57,728 and and a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Working Pattern:** Permanent \| Flexible First options available **Role Overview** As an experienced AI Product Analyst, you will join SSE’s IT Product Analysis team to lead analysis across our AI domain and support the delivery of high profile, customer facing initiatives. You’ll play a key role in driving SSE’s largescale transformation by shaping AI driven products, ensuring strong data protection practices, and guiding teams toward the highest impact opportunities. A core part of this role is staying ahead of the rapidly evolving AI landscape. You are genuinely passionate about AI, continually learning about emerging tools and technologies, and monitoring market and competitor activity to help shape our strategy. **You Will** * Lead analysis across AI and IT initiatives, delivering high\-quality product insights, requirements, documentation, and clear as\-is / to\-be process definitions. * Drive business analysis work across multiple projects, ensuring delivery within expected time, cost, and quality constraints. * Support creation of Power BI dashboards and reporting to provide performance insights and inform decision\-making. * Produce Data Protection and Compliance documentation, identifying and assessing regulatory, data, and technical risks across AI and IT initiatives. * Monitor AI market trends, competitor capabilities, and emerging tools while collaborating with Product Managers, Engineering, Data Science, and Business teams to prioritise and deliver the highest\-value work. **You Have** * Strong understanding of AI/ML fundamentals, responsible AI principles, and the ability to translate model outputs into actionable insights. Skilled in analytics, Power BI (DAX, data modelling) and Databricks. * Solid knowledge of GDPR, privacy by design, data governance, and secure data lifecycle management, ensuring all AI and analytics initiatives meet regulatory and internal compliance standards. * Experienced in writing user stories, defining requirements, documenting as\-is/to\-be processes, applying design thinking, and prioritising high impact work. Skilled in applying Agile frameworks such as Scrum and Kanban, and proficient with Azure DevOps (ADO) and modern product delivery practices. * Broad IT foundation with exposure to data pipelines, cloud platforms, system integrations, and modern application architecture * Genuine passion for AI with a clear plan to stay up to date. Actively tracks new AI tools, market trends, and competitor activity to influence forward\-looking strategy. **About SSE** SSE has a bold ambition – to be a leading energy company in a net zero world. We're investing around £10 million a day in homegrown energy to help power a cleaner, more secure future. Our investment will see us build the world's largest offshore wind farm and transform the grid to deliver greener electricity to millions. Our IT division powers growth across all SSE business areas by making sure we have the systems, software and security needed to take the lead in a low carbon world. They provide expertise, advice and day\-to\-day support in emerging technologies, data and analytics, cyber security and more. **Flexible benefits to fit your life** Enjoy discounts on private healthcare and gym memberships. Wellbeing benefits like a free online GP and 24/7 counselling service. Interest\-free loans on tech and transport season tickets, or a new bike with our Cycle to Work scheme. As well as generous family entitlements such as maternity and adoption pay, and paternity leave. **Work with an equal opportunity employer** SSE will make any reasonable adjustments you need to ensure that your application and experience with us is positive. Please contact DAvid.brickell@sse.com / 01738 275 846 to discuss how we can support you. We're dedicated to fostering an open and inclusive workplace where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We create equal opportunities for everyone to succeed and especially welcome applications from those who may not be well represented in our workforce or industry. **Ready to apply?** Start your online application using the Apply Now box on this page. We only accept applications made online. We'll be in touch after the closing date to let you know if we'll be taking your application further. If you're offered a role with SSE, you'll need to complete a criminality check and a credit check before you start work. \#LI\-DB1 \#LI\-HYBRID

title: Project Technical Engineer
description: **Job Title:** Project Technical Engineer **Company:** Distag QCS **Location:** Tullow **Reporting To:** Product Line Manager **About Distag QCS:** Distag QCS is a leading provider of components and solutions to Original Equipment Manufacturers \& Distributors. We specialize in Trailer Parts, OEM Bespoke Components, Hydraulic Systems \& Power Transmission components. **Job Description**: As a Project Technical Engineer at Distag QCS, you will play a crucial role in bridging the gap between Supplier, Customer and internal Distag Sales, Purchasing and Technical Teams, developing new products \& working on various projects mainly within our OEM Bespoke Team. You will collaborate closely with our supplier base to ensure they uphold the highest standards of product quality and performance, whilst receiving requests from our Sales team on various projects to develop new products and improve existing. **Key Responsibilities:** * Cross\-functional Collaboration: Collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams, including Sales, Procurement, Engineering \& Quality assurance to ensure alignment on supplier requirements, specifications, and expectations. * Project Management: Develop and maintain project schedules, milestones, and budgets for new product development projects. Coordinate activities across cross\-functional teams, including engineering, manufacturing, marketing, and quality assurance, to ensure timely and successful project execution. * Customer Support: Provide technical support and expertise to internal teams and customers during the product development process and after product launch. Address technical inquiries, troubleshoot issues, and implement solutions to ensure customer satisfaction. * Non\-Conformance Management. Resolve non\-conformance projects by collaboration with internal teams, suppliers and customer. * Continuous Supplier Improvement: Lead and support continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing supplier capabilities, reducing costs, and improving overall product quality and reliability. Collaborate with suppliers to implement best practices and drive process optimization. * Supplier Development: Provide guidance and support to suppliers to help them improve their processes, capabilities, and performance. * Professional Development: Stay abreast of industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in new product development. **Qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in either \-Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Business, Supply Chain Management or equivalent experience. * Previous experience in a manufacturing or engineering environment is preferred. * Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build strong relationships with suppliers and internal stakeholders \& customers. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and experience with ERP systems is preferred. * Willingness to travel to customers. Supplier visits in China \& India are not required but desired. **Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Comprehensive benefits package * Opportunities for professional development and growth * Collaborative and dynamic work environment * Chance to make a meaningful impact on product quality and customer satisfaction *Note: This job description is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the position and is not exhaustive. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change or be supplemented at the discretion of management.* Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Company events * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Tullow, County Carlow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Bachelor's (required) Experience: * Manufacturing/Engineering: 1 year (required) Work Location: In person

title: QA/Test Analyst
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you!. **Job Description** You may have been working as a Software Engineer in Test, Test Engineer or Test Analyst for a minimum of 4 years and is confident in their ability performing manual test but also enjoy conducting automation testing. You'll be part of an agile team helping our clients achieve their business transformation goals by identifying requirements, developing solutions and providing ongoing guidance and advice in test. **You May Also Get Involved With** * Defining and executing test strategies and scripts * Accurate recording and monitoring of issues * Providing support to the development of scrum teams to automate tests to guide development * Reviewing test environments and processes to facilitate continuous improvement and maintenance of quality standards * Providing test estimates **Qualifications** * Demonstrated experience of Automated Quality Assurance: Deliver overnight cross\-browser, functional, non\-functional, API, integration and acceptance tests. * Delivered front end cross\-browser functional testing, device testing, exploratory, systems integration and user acceptance testing e.g. manual testing. * Confident with building and maintaining frameworks in one or more of these languages: Java, JavaScript, Python, or C\#. * Have practical experience with open source tools such as: Cypress, Karate, PlayWright, Selenium or similar. * Worked in an agile environment. Role is hybrid with a minimum of 2\-3 days in office in Dublin city centre **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact \[recruiter email address] at Version 1\. We will consider all requests carefully, respectfully and confidentially.
